description_long: This is a rustic cabin set into a rocky, reddish terrain, with a prominent gabled roof made of dark material and a wooden facade. The structure features a central stone chimney and a cylindrical stone water tower or silo attached to one side, connected by a short walkway or bridge. The cabin is surrounded by rugged, angular rock formations that appear to be part of its foundation or immediate landscape. Windows are symmetrically placed on the front and rear facades, with a main entrance door centrally located at the front, accessible via a small platform. The exterior materials are primarily wood for the cabin, stone for the chimney and water tower, and rough rock for the base. The overall appearance suggests a blend of natural integration with the landscape and traditional cabin construction.
description: A rustic wooden cabin with a gabled roof, stone chimney, and attached cylindrical water tower, set into rugged reddish rock formations.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, central stone chimney, and symmetrical windows, with the cylindrical water tower visible to the left.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the cylindrical water tower, connecting walkway, and the rocky terrain enveloping the cabin.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facade with symmetrical windows and the continuation of the rocky base.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the wooden cabin structure, stone chimney, and the rocky foundation extending outward.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, constructed from dark material, featuring a central stone chimney
attribute_window_arrangement: Symmetrical arrangement with two medium-sized rectangular windows on the front and rear facades, evenly spaced on either side of the central door or wall section
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door centered on the front facade, accessible via a small platform
